title: leontodon protheiformis subsp. hispidus
intro: Leontodon protheiformis subsp. hispidus, also known as rough hawk's beard, is a subspecies of the dandelion-like plant Leontodon protheiformis. This plant is primarily used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, making it useful for treating wounds and skin conditions. The rough hawk's beard is believed to have several health benefits, including reducing fever, relieving digestive issues, and supporting the immune system. It can be prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, and is often used in herbal remedies for its purported medicinal properties.
